differences.title: 1. APPROACH
differences.leftDesc: Compose styles with low-level utility classes, granting maximum design freedom.
differences.rightDesc: Utilize ready-made components and styles for rapid prototyping and consistent UI.

differences.title: 2. CUSTOMIZATION
differences.leftDesc: Easily configure and extend any aspect of the design system via config file.
differences.rightDesc: Customize themes and variables, but core component structure is less adaptable.

differences.title: 3. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Requires learning utility class names, but becomes intuitive for rapid development.
differences.rightDesc: Easier to grasp core components and classes for quicker initial setup.

differences.title: 4. DESIGN SYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Build complex UIs by combining small, single-purpose utility classes.
differences.rightDesc: Leverage pre-designed, reusable UI elements like buttons, forms, and cards.

differences.title: 5. FILE SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Purges unused styles, resulting in highly optimized CSS for production.
differences.rightDesc: Includes a comprehensive set of styles, which might lead to larger initial bundles.

differences.title: 6. REACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: Apply styles directly in HTML, managing state and appearance separately.
differences.rightDesc: Component logic often dictates styling, sometimes leading to tighter coupling.

differences.title: 7. COMMUNITY
differences.leftDesc: Strong adoption in modern projects, with a vibrant and active community.
differences.rightDesc: Long-standing, massive community with extensive resources and third-party support.
